---
title: "GavinK_CodeReduction_Comments"
output: html_document
---

The below contains the **code-fold audit**. Organized by section, chunks of code that I think could be folded are listed along with my reasoning.

Sections that do not contain code that I think could be folded are excluded.

# Chapter 1: Warm-up Exercises

## 1.5 --- Multivariate scientific discoveries

#### 2D diabetes figure

***Label: fig-diabetes1***

- The narrative here is what the figure shows as opposed to its creation in R

- This is the only figure in the chapter with code shown explicitly.

# Chapter 3: Getting Started

## 3.1 --- Why plot your data?

### 3.1.2 --- One lousy point can ruin your day

#### The Davis regression figures

***Label: fig-davis-reg1***

- The narrative here is what the figure shows as opposed to its creation in R

***Label: fig-davis-reg2***

- The text describing the reversing of the `x` and `y` axes may be sufficient (removing the necessity for code to be displayed)

- A figure in Chapter 4 (label: `fig-Salaries-lm`) has similar prose before it is introduced and the code is folded. Moreover, I am thinking `fig-davis-reg2` should have its code folded for consistency.

# Chapter 4: Plots of Multivariate Data

## 4.2 --- Data Ellipses

### 4.2.1 --- Drawing data ellipses

#### The ellipses figure

***Label: fig-ellipses-coverage***

- The code for this figure is already folded; however, I am not sure if it should be. It is hard to glean what the text both directly before and after the figure is referring to without seeing the code.

### 4.2.3 --- R functions for data ellipses

#### The prestige-education figure

***Label: fig-Prestige-scatterplot-educ1***

- Wondering if this figure should have its code folded since the only change from the figure directly previous (besides font size; label: `fig-Prestige-scatterplot-income1`) is the independent variable.

## 4.7.1 --- Visual thinning

#### Scatterplot matrix of crime data

***Label: fig-crime-spm***

- I think the code can be folded since the main difference between this and previous `scatterplotMatrix()` calls (two additional arguments) is sufficiently described in the preceding text.

# 4.9 --- Generalized pairs plots

#### Code chunk to subset GGally's namespace

***Label: ggally-smooth-fns***

- I don't think this chunk adds a substantial amount to the narrative given that the functions are mentioned in-text.

# Chapter 5: Dimension Reduction

<!-- I have read up to the start of chapter 5 -->
